question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: '] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with block.number=gt:0&block.number=lt:0 (impossible range), causing extractSqlFromBlockFilters() to produce 'WHERE index > $1 AND index < $2' with both params = 0, executing a query that returns zero rows but still hits the database index?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: '] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with timestamp=gte:9999999999.999999999 (far future timestamp), causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to execute a query that returns zero rows but still performs a full index scan on consensus_end, wasting database resources?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: '] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with block.number=lte:9223372036854775807 (max long), causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to return all blocks up to the limit, effectively performing a full table scan with no meaningful filter?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: '] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with block.number=gte:0&order=asc&limit=100 and follow all pagination links to enumerate the complete block history, since there is no authentication check in getBlocks() and no rate limiting visible in the controller?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: '] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent GET /blocks requests with different timestamp ranges to perform parallel enumeration of the block history, since there is no per-user rate limiting or concurrency control visible in the BlockController?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: '] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with block.number=eq:X for every integer X from 0 to N to enumerate all blocks by number, since there is no authentication or rate limiting in getBlocks(), leaking the complete block hash chain?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: '] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks/X for every integer X from 0 to N to enumerate all blocks by number via getByHashOrNumber(), since there is no authentication check, leaking all block metadata including hash, prevHash, name, gasUsed, logsBloom, and timestamps?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: '] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a timing attack by sending GET /blocks/:hash with different hash prefixes and measuring response times, since RecordFileService.getByHashOrNumber() uses a LIKE query ('hash LIKE $1') which may have different execution times depending on whether a prefix match is found, leaking information about stored hash prefixes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: '] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with block.number=eq:X and observe whether the response is empty or contains a block, using binary search to determine the current maximum block number (chain tip) in O(log N) requests?
